articles.title: Cold mill at Bay Minette to be commissioned next quarter as project cost reaches $5B
articles.summary: Novelis' Bay Minette, Alabama greenfield plant is on track to commission its cold mill in Q3 2026, with total project cost now estimated at $5 billion, up from the original $4.1 billion figure. The 600 kt/year facility, capable of processing the equivalent of 17 billion used beverage cans annually, remains on schedule for broader commissioning in the second half of 2026.

articles.title: Aluminium can shortage hits Indian beverage makers during peak summer demand
articles.summary: Indian beverage companies are facing an aluminium can sheet shortage at the peak of summer demand, as supply chain disruptions linked to Middle East tensions tighten availability from Gulf-origin primary producers.
articles.novelis_angle: Can sheet tightness across Asian and Middle Eastern markets signals continued demand-side pressure on Novelis's beverage packaging segment — its largest end market at ~60% of shipments — and reinforces the strategic value of Novelis's closed-loop recycled content supply.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz closure fuels European, US aluminium supply concerns; regional premiums surge
articles.summary: Three months of Strait of Hormuz closure following US-Israeli strikes on Iran has driven European aluminium billet premiums to $560–600/t and briefly pushed LME aluminium above $3,700/t; a US-Iran framework agreement is expected to allow the strait to reopen around 19 June.
articles.novelis_angle: Normalisation of European P1020 premiums post-reopening would ease input cost pressure on Novelis's ten European plants, which source significant primary aluminium volumes from Gulf producers including EGA.
